brief_title: IV Artesunate Versus IV Quinine for Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria in Children
official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study Comparing Intravenous Artesunate to Intravenous Quinine for the Treatment of Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria in Children Aged 6 Months to 14 Years
interventions: Artesunate 2.4 mg/kg IV at 0, 12, 24 hours then daily until oral therapy possible
Quinine dihydrochloride 20 mg/kg loading dose then 10 mg/kg IV every 8 hours
brief_summary: This Phase 3 study compares the efficacy and safety of intravenous artesunate versus intravenous quinine for the treatment of severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in children. The primary endpoint is in-hospital mortality.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Children aged 6 months to 14 years

2. Confirmed Plasmodium falciparum infection by microscopy or rapid diagnostic test

3. At least one sign of severe malaria (WHO criteria): prostration, altered consciousness (Blantyre Coma Score < 5), respiratory distress, multiple convulsions, severe anemia (Hb < 5 g/dL), hypoglycemia (< 40 mg/dL), acidosis, jaundice with other vital organ dysfunction, or parasitemia > 10%

4. Body weight >= 5 kg

5. Parent or guardian able to provide informed consent

6. Hospitalized or being admitted for treatment

7. No IV artesunate or IV quinine given within 24 hours prior to enrollment

8. Able to receive IV medications
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Known allergy to artesunate, artemisinin derivatives, or quinine

2. Confirmed or suspected concurrent bacterial meningitis

3. Severe malnutrition (weight-for-height z-score < -3 or MUAC < 115 mm)

4. Known chronic illness (sickle cell disease, HIV on ART, chronic kidney disease)

5. Received a full course of antimalarial treatment in the past 14 days

6. Pregnancy (for post-pubertal females, urine pregnancy test required)

7. Previously enrolled in this study

inclusion_criteria.text: Confirmed Plasmodium falciparum infection by microscopy or rapid diagnostic test

At least one sign of severe malaria per WHO criteria: prostration, altered consciousness, respiratory distress, multiple convulsions, severe anemia, hypoglycemia, acidosis, jaundice, or hyperparasitemia > 10%
Parent or guardian able to provide informed consent
Hospitalized or being admitted for treatment
No IV artesunate or IV quinine given within 24 hours prior to enrollment

exclusion_criteria.text: Known allergy to artesunate, artemisinin derivatives, or quinine
Confirmed or suspected concurrent bacterial meningitis
Severe malnutrition (weight-for-height z-score < -3 or MUAC < 115 mm)
Known chronic illness (sickle cell disease, HIV on ART, chronic kidney disease)
Received a full course of antimalarial treatment in the past 14 days
Pregnancy (for post-pubertal females, urine pregnancy test required)
